run_metadata.coverage_summary: Daily incremental run. Tier 1 swept via web search across Commonwealth (AusTender) and all state/territory portals (buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders, Tenders WA, Tenders SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL). Origin fetches were broadly blocked this session (see gaps): AusTender, buy.nsw and the Tasmanian portals returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool, so opportunities were captured at headline level from search-result snippets and cross-checked across multiple queries. Seven fresh, publicly-visible, buyer-originated opportunities captured this run, spanning NSW local government and health, Tasmanian GBE and council, a Victorian water GBE (via TenderLink) and a WA water GBE market sounding. Tier 2/3 source discovery focus: three genuinely new buyer-originated sources found and recorded (TasNetworks tenders page; Greater Western Water TenderLink portal; City of Hobart tenders/EOI). Tier 3 (SAP Ariba Discovery) confirmed as registration-gated with no publicly-visible AU opportunity extractable without login; recorded as a portal source only, not fabricated as tenders.
gaps: www.tenders.gov.au (AusTender) returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run (egress/WAF block); the current ATM list and ATM detail pages could not be opened directly. No new Commonwealth opportunity was captured with confidence this run beyond items already tracked in prior runs; Commonwealth coverage was headline-level via search only.

buy.nsw.gov.au returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool; NSW opportunities (Wingecarribee internal audit, Gosford Lift 13 RFQ, HW4 Log Creek pavement RFT) were captured at headline level from search-result snippets and cross-checked, but full detail pages, documents, evaluation criteria and contacts could not be scraped - flagged entries_pending_detail.

www.tasnetworks.com.au and www.tenders.tas.gov.au both returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool; the TasNetworks TISPS RFT and City of Hobart Dorney House EOI were captured at headline level from search snippets only. Closing times, document lists and contacts not extractable this run.

SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery (Tier 3, mandatory): opportunity list is registration-gated; no publicly-visible AU RFx could be captured without an authenticated supplier session. Recorded as a portal source only, not fabricated as a tender.

Greater Western Water 'Montgomery Estate - Branch Sewer' RFT is being run by consultant Reeds Consulting on GWW's behalf and advertised via TenderLink; detail captured from a public trade-notice snippet. The TenderLink detail page itself was not opened this run - value and evaluation criteria left null.

Tier 2 sampling only: most universities/TAFEs, the majority of local councils, and health districts were not exhaustively swept this run; rotated to Tasmanian GBE/council and Victorian/WA water GBEs instead. Buying for Victoria, QTenders and Tenders SA/WA returned only known or headline-level items this run.
recommended_seed_additions: TasNetworks - Tenders (https://www.tasnetworks.com.au/tenders): Tasmanian electricity transmission/distribution GBE running RFTs (e.g. TISPS) directly and via Tenders Tasmania; not previously in the known-source list.

Greater Western Water - Current Tenders (https://portal.tenderlink.com/greaterwesternwater/alltenders/): Victorian metropolitan water GBE advertising construction works via illion TenderLink; sibling to the already-known Melbourne Water / WaterNSW / SA Water sources.
City of Hobart - Tenders & EOI (https://www.hobartcity.com.au): Tasmanian capital-city council running EOIs (e.g. Dorney House activation) via Tenders Tasmania; worth a dedicated council check.
Consider an authenticated SAP Ariba Discovery supplier account so future runs can read gated corporate RFx detail (carried from prior runs).

tenders.title: Internal Audit Program - Wingecarribee Shire Council
tenders.description: Wingecarribee Shire Council is seeking tenders from appropriately qualified and experienced contractors to undertake the Council's internal audit program to fulfil its statutory auditing and reporting obligations.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Delivery of Council's internal audit program: a multi-year schedule of internal audits across Council operations to satisfy statutory audit and risk/assurance reporting obligations, with audit findings and reports delivered to Council's audit, risk and improvement committee.
key_deliverables: Internal audit plan / program delivery across Council functions
Reporting to the Audit, Risk and Improvement Committee (ARIC)
requirements: Appropriately qualified and experienced internal audit provider (headline requirement stated in the notice; full mandatory conditions not extractable this run)
tenders.eligibility: Open to all suitably qualified and experienced contractors
tenders.submission_method: Lodge online via buy.nsw / NSW eTendering (Council also uses VendorPanel)
tenders.reasoning: Found via search of buy.nsw open tenders and confirmed against Wingecarribee Shire Council's own tenders page (RFT T-2026-15). Buyer-originated listing on the NSW government procurement portal.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only: buy.nsw returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run, so full detail page, documents, evaluation criteria and contacts could not be scraped. Closing date/time confirmed across two searches.

tenders.title: Lifecycle replacement of Lift 13 located in the P1 Carpark at Gosford Hospital
tenders.category: Building and property - Lifts and elevators
tenders.description: Central Coast Local Health District is seeking quotes for the lifecycle replacement of Lift 13 located in the P1 Carpark at Gosford Hospital.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Design, supply, installation and commissioning of a replacement passenger/vehicle lift (Lift 13) in the P1 multi-storey carpark at Gosford Hospital, including removal of the existing end-of-life lift and reinstatement, to restore reliable lift service in the carpark.
key_deliverables: Supply and installation of replacement lift
Commissioning and handover of the new lift
tenders.eligibility: Open to all suitably qualified suppliers
tenders.submission_method: Lodge online via buy.nsw / NSW eTendering
tenders.reasoning: Found via search of buy.nsw open tenders; buyer-originated listing by Central Coast Local Health District (a NSW health district) on the NSW government procurement portal.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only: buy.nsw returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool. An earlier search snippet mis-attributed the buyer to HealthShare NSW and the closing to 27-Jul; a follow-up query corrected this to Central Coast LHD, RFQ, closing 4-Aug-2026. Full detail/documents not extractable this run.

tenders.title: HW4 Log Creek Pavement Rehabilitation Stage 2 - Kerb Installation works
tenders.description: Tenders are invited for HW4 Log Creek Pavement Rehabilitation Stage 2 - Kerb Installation works.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Civil construction works to complete Stage 2 of the HW4 Log Creek pavement rehabilitation, including kerb installation, restoring the road pavement to a serviceable standard.
tenders.eligibility: Open to all suitably qualified and experienced construction contractors
tenders.submission_method: Lodge online via buy.nsw / NSW eTendering
tenders.reasoning: Found via search of buy.nsw open tenders. Buyer-originated listing on the NSW government procurement portal.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only: buy.nsw returned HTTP 403. The buyer 'HealthShare NSW' for a highway pavement/kerb job is unusual (HealthShare typically procures health goods/services) and appeared consistently in two search snippets; the buyer attribution and the exact HW4/Log Creek location should be verified on the detail page in a future run. Flagged entries_pending_detail.

tenders.title: Tasmanian Integrated System Protection Scheme (TISPS)
tenders.description: TasNetworks is seeking submissions for the Tasmanian Integrated System Protection Scheme (TISPS), with full details provided in the attached RFT document pack.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Supply and/or delivery of an integrated system protection scheme for the Tasmanian electricity network (TISPS) - the design/supply/implementation of protection and control measures to maintain power-system stability and security across the transmission network. Exact scope is in the RFT document pack, which was not accessible this run.
tenders.eligibility: Open (per public RFT notice); specific eligibility in the RFT document pack
tenders.submission_method: Via Tenders Tasmania / TasNetworks tender channel (RFT document pack)
tenders.reasoning: Found via search of Tasmanian tenders; TasNetworks is the Tasmanian electricity transmission/distribution GBE publishing this RFT directly and via Tenders Tasmania. Buyer-originated. TasNetworks recorded as a new source this run.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only: tasnetworks.com.au and tenders.tas.gov.au returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool. Closing date (13-Jul-2026) from search snippet; closing time, document pack and detailed scope not extractable this run. Flagged entries_pending_detail.

tenders.title: Dorney House - Activation and Management Opportunity
tenders.category: Property / heritage venue activation and management
tenders.description: The City of Hobart is calling expressions of interest for the activation and ongoing management of Dorney House, a heritage-listed property (and neighbouring Fort Nelson) on Porter Hill.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Proposals from private, community or government-sector operators to activate and manage the heritage-listed Dorney House site - establishing and running compatible uses/activities and taking on ongoing management, care and public activation of the heritage building and remnant fort.
key_deliverables: Proposed activation concept/uses for Dorney House (and Fort Nelson)
Ongoing site management and operation model
tenders.eligibility: Open to private, community and government-sector respondents
tenders.submission_method: Via Tenders Tasmania / City of Hobart tender channel
tenders.delivery_location: Porter Hill, Sandy Bay, Hobart, TAS
tenders.reasoning: Found via search of Tasmanian tenders; buyer-originated EOI by the City of Hobart, advertised via Tenders Tasmania. City of Hobart recorded as a new source this run.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only: Tasmanian portal returned HTTP 403. Background on Dorney House (Esmond Dorney-designed heritage house atop a former fort on Porter Hill) confirmed via architecture press; the current EOI closing 17-Jul-2026 from a Tasmanian tenders search snippet. Full EOI documents/contacts not extractable this run.

tenders.title: Montgomery Estate - Branch Sewer (610-640 Davis Road, Tarneit)
tenders.buyer: Greater Western Water (via Reeds Consulting Pty Ltd)
tenders.description: Reeds Consulting Pty Ltd, in conjunction with Greater Western Water, is releasing a Request for Tender for the Montgomery Estate Branch Sewer at 610-640 Davis Road, Tarneit, in the City of Wyndham. The works comprise construction of approximately 600 metres of DN375 and DN450 branch sewer for the developer.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Construction of approximately 600 metres of DN375 and DN450 branch sewer serving the Montgomery Estate development at 610-640 Davis Road, Tarneit (City of Wyndham), delivered to Greater Western Water accreditation standards and connected into the live sewer network.
key_deliverables: Construct ~600 m of DN375 and DN450 branch sewer
Delivery to GWW accreditation and technical standards
tenders.scope_of_work: Construction of approximately 600 metres of DN375 and DN450 branch sewer as part of the 610-640 Davis Road, Tarneit / Montgomery Estate development, for the developer, within the City of Wyndham.
requirements: Accreditation with Greater Western Water in Category SC1 (Retic/Branch Sewer <=DN280 or <=3.5m depth)
Accreditation in Category SC1(a) (Retic/Branch Sewer <=DN280 or <=5m depth)
Accreditation in Category SC2 (Branch Sewer >DN280 or Sewer Retic >5m depth)
Accreditation in Category SC7 (Live Sewer Works)
tenders.eligibility: Contractors accredited with Greater Western Water for the relevant sewer construction categories (SC1, SC1(a), SC2, SC7)
tenders.submission_method: Via Greater Western Water e-tendering (illion TenderLink portal)
mandatory_conditions: Must hold current Greater Western Water accreditation in categories SC1, SC1(a), SC2 and SC7
tenders.delivery_location: 610-640 Davis Road, Tarneit, City of Wyndham, VIC
tenders.detail_source: Public trade-notice snippet describing the RFT (contractor accreditation categories, scope, closing date and contact); GWW advertises construction contracts via illion TenderLink.
tenders.reasoning: Found via search for Greater Western Water tenders; buyer-originated (GWW, run by consultant Reeds Consulting) and advertised on GWW's own TenderLink portal. Greater Western Water TenderLink portal recorded as a new source this run.
tenders.notes: Detail (scope, sewer sizes/length, accreditation categories, contact) captured from a public trade-notice snippet; the TenderLink detail page itself requires free registration and was not opened this run, so value and evaluation criteria are null. Developer-funded works delivered to GWW standards.

tenders.title: 2026 Engineering Design Services (EDS) Panel - early tender advice
tenders.description: Water Corporation is providing early tender advice to the engineering consulting market regarding its upcoming procurement of the 2026 Engineering Design Services (EDS) Panel.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Notice of an upcoming panel procurement: Water Corporation intends to establish a 2026 Engineering Design Services (EDS) Panel to provide engineering design services across its water infrastructure program. This is early market engagement ahead of a formal approach to market; specific deliverables will be defined in the forthcoming RFT.
tenders.eligibility: Engineering consulting firms (panel to be established via forthcoming RFT)
tenders.submission_method: Forthcoming - via Tenders WA / Water Corporation when the RFT is released
tenders.reasoning: Found via Tenders WA / Water Corporation search; buyer-originated early tender advice from the WA state water GBE flagging an upcoming EDS Panel procurement. Captured as market-sounding per the prompt's guidance on early market engagement notices.
tenders.notes: Headline-level only: this is an early-engagement notice, not yet an open RFT, so there is no closing date. Recorded so the upcoming panel procurement can be tracked into a future run when the formal approach to market opens.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Found while searching Tasmanian tenders for the TISPS RFT; TasNetworks is the Tasmanian electricity transmission/distribution GBE and publishes RFTs directly on its own tenders page and via Tenders Tasmania.
discovered_sources.notes: Not previously in the known-source list. Origin returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run; sibling to other state electricity-network GBEs (Transgrid, Ausgrid, Powerlink, Western Power) already tracked.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Found while searching for the Montgomery Estate branch sewer RFT; Greater Western Water (Victorian metropolitan water GBE) advertises its construction contracts via its own illion TenderLink portal.
discovered_sources.notes: Not previously in the known-source list; sibling to the already-known Melbourne Water / WaterNSW / SA Water GBE sources. Free registration required for full opportunity detail. Also listed on Buying for Victoria (buyerId 721717).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Found via the Dorney House activation/management EOI while searching Tasmanian tenders; the City of Hobart (Tasmanian capital-city council) runs EOIs and tenders advertised via Tenders Tasmania.
discovered_sources.notes: Not previously in the known-source list. First Tasmanian council captured in the source set; worth a dedicated council check alongside the existing Tenders Tasmania coverage.

discovered_sources.source_name: SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Checked again this run to satisfy the mandatory Tier-3 sweep; the public posting board lists buyer-posted open RFx but full AU opportunity detail is registration-gated.
discovered_sources.notes: Already known. Recorded again this run to keep Tier 3 coverage explicit: no publicly-visible AU RFx could be captured without an authenticated supplier account; no corporate tenders fabricated. An authenticated session is recommended for future runs.
